title: The Libation Bearers

memes.label: "The Libation Bearers"
memes.description: "The Libation Bearers — text of the play by Aeschylus, translated by Gilbert Murray"

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Public domain dedication"

memes.label: Aegisthus' Death
memes.description: the killing of Aegisthus by Orestes, the first act of the palace vengeance — swift, unceremonious, and occurring offstage before the climactic confrontation with Clytemnestra

memes.label: Aegisthus' Death Speech
memes.description: Aegisthus confronts his killer in a brief but charged stichomythic exchange — the death of the usurper, swift and almost casual, a structural mirror to Agamemnon's murder

memes.label: Aeschylus
memes.description: Athenian tragic playwright (c. 525–456 BCE), author of the Oresteia trilogy of which The Libation Bearers is the second play.

memes.label: Agamemnon
memes.description: The murdered king of Argos, father of Orestes and Electra, whose unavenged spirit is summoned from the underworld by the libations and kommos at his tomb — the absent centre around which all the action of Aeschylus' Choephoroe revolves.

memes.label: Agamemnon (Play)
memes.description: The first play of the Oresteia, depicting Agamemnon's murder by Clytemnestra.

memes.label: Agamemnon's Tomb
memes.description: The burial mound of the murdered king — the play's central symbolic space where libations are poured, the dead are summoned, and recognition and vengeance are enacted.

memes.label: Aigisthos
memes.description: Son of Thyestes, lover of Clytemnestra, and co-murderer of Agamemnon, now ruling Argos as usurper — the first target of Orestes' vengeance in Aeschylus' Choephoroe.

memes.label: Anagnorisis (Ἀναγνώρισις)
memes.description: Anagnorisis — the moment of recognition or discovery, most famously embodied in the Recognition Scene where Electra identifies Orestes by the lock of hair, footprint, and weaving.

memes.label: Apollo
memes.description: Apollo (Loxias) — the Olympian god of prophecy and purification, whose command from Delphi compels Orestes to avenge Agamemnon by killing Clytemnestra, and whose divine authority frames the central moral crisis of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Apollo and Athena
memes.description: the two Olympian deities who together resolve the Oresteia's crisis — Apollo commanding the vengeance, Athena transforming it into civic justice

memes.label: Apollo and the Oresteia
memes.description: Apollo's role across the Oresteia trilogy — from commanding Orestes' matricide in *The Libation Bearers* to defending him at the Areopagus in *The Eumenides*

memes.label: Apollo Loxias
memes.description: Apollo in his prophetic aspect as "Loxias" — the god who commanded Orestes to kill his mother, promised purification, and whose Delphic oracle is central to the Oresteia's religious framework.

memes.label: Argos
memes.description: The city of Argos, seat of the House of Atreus and the setting of the Choephoroe — a palace that has become both home and prison, throne-room and slaughterhouse.

memes.label: Atê (Ἄτη)
memes.description: Atê — divine blindness or ruin sent by the gods, the delusion that drives mortals toward their own destruction, a central religious concept in Aeschylus' Oresteia.

memes.label: Athena
memes.description: The goddess of wisdom and patron of Athens, who casts the deciding vote at Orestes' trial in the *Eumenides* and transforms the Furies from agents of private vengeance to guardians of civic justice.

memes.label: Atreus
memes.description: King of Mycenae, father of Agamemnon and Menelaus. His sin against his brother Thyestes — feeding him his own children — established the ancestral curse that drives the Oresteia's cycle of vengeance.

memes.label: Blood and Ritual Impurity
memes.description: The Greek religious concept that spilled blood creates a ritual stain (miasma) requiring purification, central to the moral logic of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Blood-Guilt
memes.description: The ritual pollution (miasma) incurred by the shedding of kindred blood, which requires purification and drives the vengeance cycle in Aeschylus' Choephoroe.

memes.label: Blood Guilt and Purification
memes.description: the religious concept of miasma (pollution) that adheres to the killer and can only be cleansed through ritual — central to Orestes' dilemma and Apollo's promise of purification

memes.label: Blood Pollution and Purification
memes.description: the ritual concept of blood pollution (miasma) and its purification — Orestes' double bind, compelled to shed kindred blood yet requiring cleansing from the very act

memes.label: Cassandra
memes.description: Cassandra — the Trojan prophetess, daughter of Priam, brought to Argos as Agamemnon's captive and killed alongside him by Clytemnestra; her unheeded prophetic vision in the Agamemnon resonates through The Libation Bearers as a shadow of truths spoken and ignored.

memes.label: Catharsis (Κάθαρσις)
memes.description: The Greek concept of ritual purification and cleansing — the process by which the stain of blood-guilt is removed, central to the Oresteia's resolution in Eumenides.

memes.label: Choral Ode
memes.description: The choral ode — a formal lyric composition structured in alternating strophe and antistrophe — through which the Chorus of The Libation Bearers interprets the action, invokes the dead, and voices the play's theological vision.

memes.label: The Chorus's Final Justice Hymn
memes.description: The Chorus's closing hymn celebrating the fall of the usurpers and the deliverance of the House of Atreus, followed by a meditation on the cycle of violence in the race

memes.label: Clytemnestra
memes.description: Wife of Agamemnon, mother of Orestes and Electra, who with her lover Aigisthos murdered Agamemnon upon his return from Troy and now rules Argos — the target of her son's divinely commanded vengeance in Aeschylus' Choephoroe.

memes.label: Ho, there, mine axe of battle!
memes.description: Upon hearing Aegisthus is slain, Clytemnestra calls for her battle axe — a flash of the formidable warrior-queen from Agamemnon, revealing her readiness to face death on her own terms in The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Clytemnestra's Confrontation
memes.description: The climactic stichomythia between Orestes and Clytemnestra before the matricide — a line-by-line duel of accusation and plea

memes.label: Clytemnestra's Death
memes.description: the climactic murder of Clytemnestra by her son Orestes — the matricide that is simultaneously the fulfilment of Apollo's command and the most abhorrent crime in Greek thought

memes.label: Clytemnestra's Dream
memes.description: The prophetic dream sent to Clytemnestra in Aeschylus' Choephoroe — in which she nurses a serpent that draws blood from her breast — a divine omen that both reveals her guilt and foretells her death at Orestes' hands.

memes.label: Clytemnestra's Dream Speech
memes.description: Clytemnestra describes the prophetic dream of giving birth to a serpent — the dream that drives the action of The Libation Bearers

memes.label: Clytemnestra's False Grief
memes.description: Clytemnestra's public performance of grief upon hearing of Orestes' "death" — a masterclass in dramatic irony where she grieves publicly while privately relieved
aliases: ah me so cometh the last wreck

memes.label: Clytemnestra's False Welcome
memes.description: Clytemnestra welcomes the disguised Orestes with elaborate courtesy — a masterpiece of dramatic irony in which every word of welcome is freighted with impending doom

memes.label: Clytemnestra's Lament for Orestes
memes.description: Clytemnestra's reaction upon hearing the (false) news of Orestes' death — a masterpiece of dramatic irony as she laments her son's death to his disguised face.

memes.label: Curse of the House of Atreus
memes.description: The inherited curse upon the royal house of Argos, tracing from Atreus through Agamemnon to Orestes, which shapes the generational cycle of kin-murder in Aeschylus' Choephoroe.

memes.label: The Cycle of Violence
memes.description: The self-perpetuating chain of kin-murder that defines the House of Atreus.

memes.label: Darkness and Light
memes.description: The recurring pattern of light-versus-darkness imagery in The Libation Bearers, mapping moral clarity against spiritual blindness and the movement from the shadowed House of Atreus toward eventual light.

memes.label: Death of Aigisthos
memes.description: Orestes kills Aigisthos, his father's usurper and co-murderer, at the palace before turning to confront Clytemnestra

relations.label: Curse of the House of Atreus

memes.label: Deliverance and Salvation
memes.description: The theological concept of deliverance (sōtēria) in Aeschylus' Oresteia — the movement from crime through punishment to salvation — which gives the trilogy its overarching religious shape and transforms the Erinyes from Furies to Beneficent Beings.

memes.label: Delphi
memes.description: The sanctuary of Apollo at Delphi, whose oracle commands Orestes to avenge his father — a distant sacred space whose authority drives the play's action.

memes.label: Dike (Δίκη)
memes.description: The Greek concept of justice, righteous judgment, and retribution — the second element in the Oresteia's central triad of Hubris, Dike, and Soteria.

memes.label: Divine Justice and Human Law
memes.description: the conflict between the ancient chthonic law of blood-vengeance (the Furies' domain) and the Olympian civic justice (Apollo and Athena's domain) — the central theological argument of the Oresteia

memes.label: Divine Vengeance
memes.description: The religious and moral framework of retaliatory justice that drives the action of Aeschylus' Choephoroe, in which kin-murder demands blood-for-blood repayment under divine sanction.

memes.label: Dramatic Irony
memes.description: the gap between what characters know and what the audience knows — pervasive in the recognition scene, the false death report, and the final confrontation — a key formal device of Greek tragedy

memes.label: Electra
memes.description: Daughter of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra, sister of Orestes, who leads the Chorus of Libation-Bearers in prayer at her father's tomb and is reunited with her exiled brother in Aeschylus' Choephoroe.

memes.label: Electra's Isolation
memes.description: Electra's account of her outcast status in her father's house — "Like a dog they drove me back, and the door was shut" — a devastating image of the dispossessed princess during the great kommos at the tomb
aliases: like a dog they drove me back

memes.label: "Electra's Prayer at the Tomb"
memes.description: Electra's anguished prayer at Agamemnon's tomb — torn between duty to mourn, hatred of the murderers who sent the offerings, and the desire for justice.

memes.label: Electra's Prayer Tutorial
memes.description: The stichomythia between Electra and the Chorus Leader teaching her how to pray for vengeance at Agamemnon's tomb — culminating in the Leader's instruction: "one who shall slay the slayer."
aliases: how to pray at the tomb
one who shall slay the slayer

memes.label: Electra's Recognition Cry
memes.description: Electra's ecstatic cry upon recognizing Orestes — the emotional climax of the recognition scene, overflowing with joy and relief at her brother's return

memes.label: Eumenides (Play)
memes.description: The final play of the Oresteia, resolving the crisis left by the Choephoroe.

memes.label: Euripides
memes.description: Athenian tragic playwright (c. 480–406 BCE) whose Electra (413 BCE) provides a psychological re-working of the Orestes myth in deliberate counterpoint to Aeschylus's treatment.

memes.label: Euripides' Electra
memes.description: Euripides' Electra (c. 413 BCE), a psychological re-working of the Orestes-Electra myth that provides the most sustained ancient counterpoint to Aeschylus's treatment in The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Exile and Return
memes.description: The narrative pattern of exile and return (nostos) that structures Orestes' story in Aeschylus' Choephoroe — his childhood expulsion from Argos, his years in Phocis, and his climactic return to reclaim his father's throne through vengeance.

memes.label: Family and Kinship
memes.description: the bonds of blood — between father and son, mother and child, brother and sister — that are both the source of deepest obligation and the arena of most terrible violence in the play

memes.label: Grief and Lamentation Ritual
memes.description: The ritualized expression of grief through song, gesture, and offering at the tomb, forming the dramatic and emotional foundation of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Guile and Deception
memes.description: the play's action depends on deception — Orestes' disguise as a Phocian stranger, Clytemnestra's false grief, the Chorus's altered message to the Nurse — all instruments of a justice that cannot speak its name

memes.label: Guile and Justice
memes.description: The use of deception (dolos) as an instrument of divine justice in Aeschylus' Choephoroe — the principle that those who killed by guile shall themselves be killed by guile, transforming a morally ambiguous means into a divinely sanctioned method.

memes.label: Hermes
memes.description: Hermes — the messenger god and guide of souls (Psychopompos), invoked in the opening lines of The Libation Bearers as "Son of Zeus Chthonios" and warder of the underworld, whose presence frames the play's central concern with communication between the living and the dead.

memes.label: Homer
memes.description: Homer — the legendary epic poet whose Odyssey provides the earliest surviving Oresteia narrative, referenced throughout Murray's Preface to The Libation Bearers as the archaic source against which Aeschylus' tragic transformation is measured.

memes.label: House of Atreus
memes.description: The royal palace of the Atreidae dynasty in Argos — the physical locus of the family curse, where Agamemnon was murdered and where Orestes returns to kill Clytemnestra and Aigisthos.

memes.label: Hubris (ὕβρις)
memes.description: The Greek concept of overweening pride, insolence, or violent transgression — the first element in the Oresteia's central religious triad of Hubris, Dike, and Soteria.

memes.label: Inachos
memes.description: The river god of Argos, invoked by Orestes in his opening prayer at Agamemnon's tomb. The Inachos was one of the "life-giving" rivers worshipped in drought-prone Argos.

memes.label: Invocation of the Dead
memes.description: The ritual summoning of the dead at the grave, central to Aeschylus' Choephoroe, in which libations, prayers, and lamentation awaken Agamemnon's spirit to aid his children's vengeance.

memes.label: Iphigenia
memes.description: Daughter of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra, sacrificed by her father at Aulis to appease Artemis and allow the Greek fleet to sail for Troy. Her death is the background cause of Clytemnestra's vengeance against Agamemnon.

memes.label: Justice and Vengeance
memes.description: The central thematic conflict of the Oresteia — whether justice can be distinguished from vengeance, and how blood-vengeance might be transformed into civic legal order.

memes.label: Justice vs. Vengeance
memes.description: The central thematic tension of the Oresteia: is Orestes' killing of his mother an act of justice or merely another act of vengeance in an endless cycle?

memes.label: Killing of Clytemnestra
memes.description: The climactic event of Aeschylus' Choephoroe — Orestes' killing of his mother Clytemnestra after she bares her breast and pleads for mercy, the act that simultaneously fulfils divine vengeance and generates the pollution that drives the Eumenides.

memes.label: Kommos (Κομμός)
memes.description: The Greek dramatic form of antiphonal ritual lamentation between actors and chorus — the structural and emotional center of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Law of Retribution
memes.description: The archaic moral principle that governs justice in Aeschylus' Choephoroe — "on him that doeth shall be done" — which Aeschylus probes and ultimately transcends through the trilogy's movement toward civic law.

memes.label: The Libation Ritual
memes.description: The ritual pouring of offerings at Agamemnon's tomb — the namesake act of the play — which Clytemnestra intends as appeasement but which Electra transforms into a summons for vengeance.

memes.label: Madness and Divine Possession
memes.description: the Furies' pursuit of Orestes manifests as madness — a state of divine possession that is both punishment and revelation, blurring the line between sanity and divine affliction

memes.label: Memory and Mourning
memes.description: the role of ritual lamentation, tomb cult, and the persistence of memory in sustaining the demand for vengeance across generations — embodied in the great kommos at Agamemnon's tomb

memes.label: Moira (Μοῖρα)
memes.description: The Greek concept of fate, destiny, or allotted portion — the cosmic force that apportions to each being their share of life, suffering, and death.

memes.label: Murder of Agamemnon
memes.description: The killing of Agamemnon by Clytemnestra and Aigisthos upon his return from Troy — the originating crime that drives the entire action of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Oath and Duty
memes.description: The binding religious obligation — enforced by oaths sworn to the gods and the dead — that compels Orestes to avenge his father despite the moral horror of matricide in Aeschylus' Choephoroe.

memes.label: Omen and Prophecy
memes.description: Omen and Prophecy — the system of divine signs, dreams, and oracular utterances that drives the action of The Libation Bearers, from Clytemnestra's serpent dream to Apollo's Delphic command and the Chorus's prophetic invocations.

memes.label: "On Him That Doeth Shall Be Done"
memes.description: The Chorus's declaration of the ancient law of retributive justice — "On him that doeth shall be done" — the play's most concentrated statement of the blood-vengeance principle.

memes.label: Oresteia
memes.description: The only complete tragic trilogy surviving from ancient Athens, produced in 458 BCE.

memes.label: Orestes
memes.description: Son of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra, brother of Electra, the protagonist of Aeschylus' Choephoroe who returns from exile to avenge his father's murder by killing his mother and her lover Aigisthos.

memes.label: Orestes as Saviour
memes.description: The dramatic irony of Orestes' title — he is called "Saviour" by the Chorus and invokes Hermes as "Saviour," yet his act of salvation is matricide, and he ends the play "as one Accursed, never to rest again."

memes.label: Orestes the Broken Charioteer
memes.description: Orestes' first moment of madness after the matricide — "I steer unseeing, like some broken charioteer, / By curbless visions borne" — the onset of the Furies' pursuit.

memes.label: Orestes' Disguise Plan
memes.description: Orestes' detailed plan to enter the palace disguised as a Phocian traveler — the strategic deception that sets the revenge plot in motion, combining practical cunning with divine sanction
aliases: first i array me in a strangers guise

memes.label: O Zeus, look down on our estate — the eagle's brood
memes.description: Orestes' appeal to Zeus comparing himself and Electra to orphaned eaglets whose father has been trapped and killed — the defining Aeschylean metaphor of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Orestes' Exile
memes.description: the aftermath of the matricide — Orestes flees the palace pursued by the Furies, his reason disintegrating, a Saviour become Accursed, never to rest again

memes.label: Orestes' Farewell to the Palace
memes.description: Orestes' final speech before going into exile — prophesying the Furies' pursuit and his own suffering to come, forming the transition to The Eumenides.

memes.label: They are here — like Gorgons, gowned in darkness
memes.description: Orestes' first hallucination of the Furies after the matricide — he sees them as Gorgon-like figures in dark robes with serpents, the climactic vision that drives him from Argos and sets the stage for The Eumenides.

memes.label: Orestes' Opening Prayer
memes.description: Orestes' opening invocation to Hermes at Agamemnon's tomb — the first words of Aeschylus' The Libation Bearers, establishing the themes of return, vengeance, and communication with the dead.

memes.label: Orestes' Phocian Tale
memes.description: Orestes' elaborate fabricated story about being a Phocian merchant bringing news of his own death — the deception that gains him entry to the palace and seals Clytemnestra's fate.

memes.label: Orestes' Return
memes.description: Orestes' return from exile in Phokis to Argos, the initiating event of The Libation Bearers that sets the revenge plot in motion.

memes.label: "Orestes' Revelation of Apollo's Command"
memes.description: Orestes' speech revealing the full horror of Apollo's command — the god's threat of what awaits him if he fails to kill his mother, delivered with increasing terror at the tomb.

memes.label: Orestes' Revenge Vow
memes.description: Orestes swears by Zeus to avenge his father's murder — the moment the ritual lament at Agamemnon's tomb transforms into a pact of divine vengeance

memes.label: Peripeteia (Περιπέτεια)
memes.description: Peripeteia — the sudden reversal of fortune or circumstances, a key dramatic concept in Greek tragedy most powerfully embodied in The Libation Bearers by Clytemnestra's swift fall from queen to corpse at the hands of the son she thought safely distant.

memes.label: Purification Rite
memes.description: The ritual process of cleansing blood-guilt (katharmos), which in Aeschylus' Choephoroe is sought through libations and ultimately achieved through divine intervention and the establishment of law.

memes.label: Pylades
memes.description: Son of Strophius of Phocis, the silent companion of Orestes who speaks only once in Aeschylus' Choephoroe — but that single speech, recalling Apollo's command, determines the play's climactic moment when Orestes wavers before killing his mother.

memes.label: Pylades' Intervention
memes.description: Pylades' only spoken lines in the entire Oresteia — three brief lines at the critical moment of Orestes' hesitation before the matricide, reminding him of Apollo's command.

memes.label: Recognition and Identity in the Oresteia
memes.description: the anagnorisis (recognition) scene between Orestes and Electra — one of the great recognition scenes in Greek tragedy, employing tokens, reasoning, and emotional reunion

memes.label: Recognition and Tokens
memes.description: The dramatic device of recognition (anagnorisis) through physical tokens — the lock of hair, footprints, and woven cloak — by which Orestes and Electra are reunited in Aeschylus' Choephoroe, a scene later refined by Sophocles and Euripides.

memes.label: The Recognition Scene
memes.description: Electra discovers Orestes through the lock of hair, his footprints, and the woven cloak at Agamemnon's tomb — the scene that reunites the siblings and authorizes the vengeance plot.

memes.label: Revenge and Justice
memes.description: the central thematic dialectic of the Oresteia — the irreducible tension between personal vengeance and impersonal justice that drives the trilogy's action

memes.label: Ritual Lamentation
memes.description: The formalised ritual lament (kommos) in Greek tragedy — an antiphonal exchange between actors and chorus — which in Aeschylus' Choephoroe becomes the dramatic vehicle for summoning the dead and galvanising vengeance.

memes.label: Sacrifice and Offering
memes.description: Ritual sacrifice and funerary offering — the pouring of libations (χοαί) that gives the play its title and structures the central ritual action at Agamemnon's tomb.

memes.label: Skylla
memes.description: the sea monster Scylla (Σκύλλα) — invoked in the play's imagery as a figure of consuming destruction, part of the mythological language the Chorus uses to express the horror of the House of Atreus' crimes

memes.label: Slavery and Freedom
memes.description: the tension between the Chorus's status as captive slaves and their role as moral agents, and the broader theme of freedom from tyranny that Orestes' vengeance promises to restore to Argos

memes.label: Sophocles
memes.description: Sophocles — the Athenian tragedian (c. 497–406 BCE) whose Electra provides the most important comparative treatment of the Orestes myth, frequently referenced in Gilbert Murray's Introduction and Endnotes to The Libation Bearers for its deliberate suppression of the religious problem in favour of direct dramatic passion.

memes.label: Sophocles' Electra
memes.description: Sophocles' Electra (date unknown), which suppresses the religious problem of matricide to focus on the elements of direct passion — a deliberate alternative to Aeschylus's theological treatment.

memes.label: Stichomythia
memes.description: A formal feature of Greek tragedy — rapid-fire line-by-line dialogue exchange between two characters. Several key scenes in *The Libation Bearers* employ stichomythia to heighten dramatic tension.

memes.label: Supplication (Ἱκεσία)
memes.description: Hiketeia — the ritual act of supplication, a formal entreaty for divine or human protection, structuring key moments of prayer and asylum-seeking in The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: The Areopagus
memes.description: the hill of Ares in Athens, where the first homicide court was established by Athena and where Orestes is tried in the *Eumenides* — the site that transforms private vengeance into civic justice

memes.label: The Chorus of Libation-Bearers
memes.description: The Chorus of foreign slave-women who give Aeschylus' Choephoroe its name — they accompany Electra to Agamemnon's tomb, guide her prayer, participate in the great kommos, and serve as the play's moral and ritual consciousness.

memes.label: The Chorus's Battle Prayer
memes.description: The Chorus's urgent prayer to Zeus moments before Aegisthus enters and is killed — "Zeus, Zeus, how shall I speak, and how / Begin to pray thee and beseech?" — the tension peaks as the blades "creep forth to battle."

memes.label: The Chorus's catalogue of monstrous women
memes.description: The Chorus's paradeigma of mythical female criminals — Althaea who burned her son's brand, Scylla who betrayed her father, and the Lemnian women — building toward Clytemnestra as the ultimate example of transgressive femininity in The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: The Chorus Instructs the Nurse
memes.description: The Chorus subverts Clytemnestra's message, instructing the Nurse to tell Aegisthus to come "alone, quick, fearing nothing" — a pivotal intervention that ensures Aegisthus arrives unguarded and meets his death.

memes.label: The Chorus's Justice Ode
memes.description: The Chorus reflects on justice, the Erinyes, and the law of blood-for-blood — a central choral meditation on the play's themes of retribution and divine order

memes.label: The Chorus's Lament for Agamemnon
memes.description: The Chorus opens the play with a lament for the dead Agamemnon, setting the tone of grief and suppressed rage that pervades The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: The Chorus's Opening Ode (Parodos)
memes.description: The Chorus's first great ode opening the play — a meditation on the prophetic dream, blood guilt, slavery, and the irreversibility of shed blood that sets the theological framework of The Libation Bearers

memes.label: The Chorus's Prayer to Zeus
memes.description: The Chorus's tense prayer for divine assistance moments before the killing of Aegisthus — the women's urgent appeal to Zeus as Orestes stands alone against two usurpers inside the palace.

memes.label: The Curse of the House of Atreus
memes.description: The inherited guilt and divine punishment that passes through generations of the House of Atreus — from Atreus to Agamemnon to Orestes — forming the tragic framework of the Oresteia.

memes.label: The Electra Scene
memes.description: the recognition (anagnorisis) between Orestes and Electra at Agamemnon's tomb — one of the great recognition scenes in Greek tragedy, employing tokens of identity and culminating in the siblings' joint resolve

memes.label: The Furies (Erinyes)
memes.description: The Furies (Erinyes) — the chthonic spirits of vengeance who pursue kin-murderers, transformed at the trilogy's end from Furies to Eumenides ("Beneficent Beings"), embodying the Oresteia's central religious argument: that the archaic law of blood-vengeance must be transmuted into civic justice.

memes.label: The Great Kommos
memes.description: The extended ritual lamentation at Agamemnon's tomb — the structural and emotional centre of Aeschylus' Choephoroe — in which Orestes, Electra, and the Chorus alternate in summoning the dead king and resolving upon vengeance.

memes.label: The House of Atreus
memes.description: the royal dynasty of Argos, spanning from Tantalus and Pelops through Atreus and Thyestes to Agamemnon and Orestes — the lineage whose crimes and vengeance structure the Oresteia

memes.label: The Kommos
memes.description: The great kommos at Agamemnon's tomb — the central ritual scene of *The Libation Bearers* in which Orestes, Electra, and the Chorus raise a collective lament that transforms grief into resolve and sets the vengeance in motion.

memes.label: The Kommos United Cry
memes.description: The joint cry of Orestes, Electra, and the Chorus at the climax of the kommos — "O great King, hear us. Awake thee to the sun. Be with us against thy foe!" — the moment when the living and dead unite in purpose.

memes.label: The Mother-Murder
memes.description: The central moral crisis of Aeschylus' Choephoroe — Orestes' killing of his mother Clytemnestra — which Aeschylus presents as simultaneously a sin, a duty, and the necessary act through which the trilogy's religious problem is confronted.

memes.label: The Mother-Murder Speech
memes.description: Orestes' defense of the matricide to the Chorus — invoking Apollo's command and the justice of killing Clytemnestra, the central moral argument of the play.

memes.label: The Net and the Robe
memes.description: The Net and the Robe — the central symbolic object of the Oresteia, the bloodstained robe-net in which Clytemnestra trapped and killed Agamemnon, displayed by Orestes at the climax of The Libation Bearers as material evidence of the crime and visual token of the vengeance cycle.

memes.label: The Nets of Agamemnon
memes.description: Orestes displays the robe in which Clytemnestra trapped and killed Agamemnon — the physical evidence of the crime, a theatrical coup that brings the hidden murder into visible, undeniable presence

memes.label: The Nurse
memes.description: Orestes' childhood nurse (Cilissa) in Aeschylus' Choephoroe, whose genuine grief at the false news of his death provides a moment of unfeigned humanity amid the play's relentless vengeance, and whose message to Aigisthos is deliberately altered by the Chorus to ensure his death.

memes.label: The Nurse's Grief
memes.description: Cilissa the Nurse mourns the false news of Orestes' death — a rare moment of ordinary, non-heroic human feeling in Greek tragedy that contrasts sharply with the royal family's cycle of violence

memes.label: The Nurse's Speech
memes.description: Cilissa the Nurse's famous monologue about nursing the infant Orestes — a rare humanizing moment in Greek tragedy, contrasting earthy domestic love with the royal family's cycle of violence.

memes.label: The Oresteia
memes.description: Aeschylus' great trilogy — *Agamemnon*, *The Libation Bearers* (Choephoroe), and *The Eumenides* — produced in 458 BCE, the only surviving Greek tragic trilogy. It traces the transformation of private vengeance into civic justice.

memes.label: The Persae
memes.description: Aeschylus' earlier tragedy *The Persae* (472 BCE), produced fourteen years before the Oresteia — compared with *The Libation Bearers* in the Preface for its treatment of divine justice and its position in the development of Aeschylean dramaturgy

memes.label: The Recognition Tokens
memes.description: Electra discovers the lock of hair, footprints, and weaving that prove Orestes has returned — the famous recognition tokens scene

memes.label: The Serpent That I Bare and Fed
memes.description: Clytemnestra's cry of recognition as Orestes drives her toward death — "The serpent that I bare and fed!" — the dream's prophecy fulfilled in the moment of matricide.

memes.label: Treason! Our master! Treason!
memes.description: The Household Slave rushes out crying "Treason! Our master! Treason!" — a brief but explosive moment of offstage death notification that shifts the dramatic tension and alerts Clytemnestra in The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: The Tomb of Agamemnon
memes.description: the grave mound at which the entire first half of *The Libation Bearers* takes place — the ritual center of the play, where mourning becomes vengeance and the dead are summoned to aid the living

memes.label: The Trilogy Form
memes.description: The connected tragic trilogy — the distinctive dramatic form of which Aeschylus' Oresteia is the only complete surviving example — in which three plays develop a single religious and moral problem across an arc of crime, punishment, and deliverance.

memes.label: The Trilogy Storm Finale
memes.description: The Chorus's final passage summarizing the three great storms that have shaken the House of Atreus across the generations — the closing words of the play that bridge to The Eumenides.

memes.label: Thyestes
memes.description: Brother of Atreus and father of Aigisthos, whose children were slaughtered and served to him by Atreus at the infamous "Banquet of Thyestes" — the originating crime of the curse that haunts the House of Atreus across the Oresteia.

memes.label: Tragic Irony
memes.description: Tragic irony — the dramatic technique in which the audience knows what characters do not — pervades The Libation Bearers, from Orestes' disguised return with news of his own death to Clytemnestra's fatal misreading of every omen.

memes.label: Troy
memes.description: The city of Troy (Ilium) in Asia Minor, whose ten-year siege by the Greeks provides the mythological backdrop and origin point for the Oresteia story.

memes.label: The Women's Quarters
memes.description: The Women's Quarters of the Argive palace — a distinct dramatic space in The Libation Bearers, entered through a separate door on the skene

memes.label: Xenia (Ξενία)
memes.description: Xenia — the sacred Greek institution of guest-friendship, whose violation by Aigisthos and ironic deployment by Orestes structures the deception scene of The Libation Bearers.

memes.label: Zeus
memes.description: Zeus — king of the Olympian gods and the ultimate guarantor of cosmic justice (Dike) in Aeschylus' Oresteia, repeatedly invoked throughout The Libation Bearers as the divine power who weighs human deeds and ordains the balance of crime and punishment.
